free_text: Dipole strength in 80Se for s process and nuclear
transmutation of 79Se
JEIN Institute for Fundamental Science, Kyoto
Hashemite University, Zarqa
gammaELBE
Ee=11.5 MeV (average current of ~710 uA) on Nb
foil (7 um thick) with an Al absorber (10 cm thick)
behind the radiator to reduce the low-energy part of
the bremsstrahlung spectrum.
1952.9 mg enriched 80Se disk (2 cm in diam.)
200.0 mg enriched 11B disk (2 cm in diam.) for
flux determination
Efficiency of ~100% relative to a NaI detector
(7.6 cm diam., 7.6 cm long).
One HPGe detector at 90 deg relative to the photon
beam direction and at distance of 28 cm from the
target.
Two HPGe detectors at 127 deg relative to the photon
beam direction and at distance of 32 cm from the
target.
Absorbers (8 mm Pb+3 mm Cu at 90 deg, 3 mm Pb+3 mm Cu
at 127 deg) were placed in front of HPGe detectors
to reduce the count rate due to low-energy photons.
All HPGe detectors were surrounded by BGO
scintillation detectors (3 cm thick).
No information on the source of uncertainty
Proof-read by Ayano Makinaga (2016-10-14)
On
90 deg/127 deg intensity ratio
Measured at 127 deg
J-pi=2+ assigned by B.Singh et al.(2005)
Spin assignment uncertain
Spin-parity of 666.3, 1449.4 and 1959.8 keV levels
given
Table I of Phys.Rev.C94(2016)044304
Absorption (scattering) cross section was derived by
dividing the (g,g0) elastic scattering cross section
by the ground state transition branching ratio.
Selection of ground state transitions and estimation
of ground state transition branching ratios were done
by simulating gamma-ray cascades by the Monte Carlo
code gammaDEX with the level schemes obtained by the
code gammaDEX.
Table II of Phys.Rev.C94(2016)044304
